name: Readwise
price_basis: Full at $9.99 per month billed annually ($12.99 monthly); Lite at $5.59 per month billed annually, checked 2026-07
verdict_reason: TRIM: keep the Kindle highlight sync on Lite at $5.59. Zo is the reading queue. $52.80 a year back.
what_zo_builds: The reading half of Readwise: a save-to-workspace flow for articles, an RSS pull for the feeds you follow, and a daily note that quotes your own highlights back to you. The Kindle and Kobo highlight sync stays on the Lite plan, because that plumbing is genuinely theirs. Zo replaces the Reader app, not the sync.
dont_kill_if: Reader is your single reading surface across newsletters, PDFs, and YouTube, and the daily review drives real retention practice. Then the Full plan is the product, not a bundle.
build_brief: Goal: downgrade Readwise Full to Lite and let Zo be the reading queue. Export my Reader library and feeds. Point the feeds at a Zo RSS pull, add a save route for new articles, and store full text in the workspace. Build a daily note that resurfaces a handful of my own highlights, mirroring the review habit. Keep Lite for Kindle highlight sync. Run it for two weeks, then downgrade. Done when saving, reading, and daily review all happen in my workspace and the plan shows Lite.
